turns.name: JOHN ROBERTS
turns.text: Justice Gorsuch has the opinion of the court this morning in case 21-887 Perez v. Sturgis Public Schools.

turns.name: NEIL GORSUCH
turns.text: The Individuals with Disabilities Education Act or IDEA promises disabled children a free and appropriate public education. Other federal laws like the Americans with Disabilities Act or the ADA provide disabled children with additional protections. This case raises the question when children with disabilities in their families may sue under other laws like the ADA without first exhausting IDEA's administrative processes. Some lower court judges have held that is a general rule. Individuals who seek to bring claims under federal laws like the ADA must exhaust all of IDEA's administrative procedures. Others have read the law more narrowly suggesting that individuals must exhaust IDEA's procedures only if they seek remedies under federal laws like the ADA that are also available under IDEA. Who has the better reading of the law holds important consequences from Mr. Perez's family and from any other families with disabled children like them. For the reasons detailed in the opinion filed with the Court today, we agree with the narrower reading of the law. As we read the relevant statutory provisions, individuals like Mr. Perez do not need to exhaust IDEA's administrative procedures before they may bring claims under other federal laws seeking remedies that IDEA cannot supply. Here that means that Mr. Perez is free to pursue his ADA claim because the relief he seeks in that lawsuit compensatory damages is not something IDEA can furnish. The judgment of the Sixth Circuit is reversed. The decision of the Court is unanimous.
